articles.title: Ebola scare: B'luru woman arriving from Uganda quarantined, govt on alert; what we know so far
articles.description: A traveler returning from Uganda has been isolated in Bengaluru with mild body ache, prompting heightened surveillance by Indian authorities. While no confirmed Ebola case has been detected in India, the individual is undergoing testing as a precautionary measure amid ongoing outbreaks in Africa.

articles.title: Indian Navy foils possible piracy attack near merchant vessel in Western Indian Ocean
articles.description: INS Kolkata thwarted a potential pirate attack on merchant vessel MV Mashallah 1 in the Western Indian Ocean. The Indian Navy warship, on anti-piracy patrol, swiftly deployed its helicopter for surveillance and conducted boarding operations, ensuring the safety of the ship and its crew. This intervention highlights the Navy's commitment to protecting vital shipping lanes.

articles.title: 'That's a terrible question': Phillips loses cool during presser after defeat
articles.description: Glenn Phillips reacted angrily after Gujarat Titans’ 92-run loss to RCB in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. When a reporter asked if GT had mentally given up while chasing 255, Phillips called it a “silly” and “terrible” question, insisting professional cricketers never quit. He admitted scoreboard pressure hurt GT after early wickets, but said the team must now stay calm and prepare for the Eliminator.

articles.title: The urge to purge: In Trump’s GOP, disloyalty is political death
articles.description: Republican politicians face a stark choice: unwavering loyalty to Donald Trump or political oblivion. Recent primary defeats, like Senator John Cornyn's, underscore Trump's absolute control. Conservatism alone is insufficient; submission to Trump is paramount. This dynamic forces GOP figures to prioritize his approval, even as national polls show unease, highlighting a deep ideological divide within the party.

articles.title: "Worried about getting sued": HasanAbi accuses Fox News of running a “stalker operation” during heated livestream
articles.description: Twitch streamer HasanAbi criticized Fox News after the network referenced the 2025 "CollarGate" controversy involving his dog, Kaya, during a segment about his federal subpoena related to a trip to Cuba. Reacting on stream, Hasan accused the network of employing “literal stalkers” and called its coverage a “stalker operation.” He argued that evidence had already disproven key allegations and expressed frustration over the controversy being revived months later.

articles.title: Stock Market Live Updates Today: BSE Sensex, Nifty50 open flat in trade on mixed global cues; Asian stocks rise
articles.full_text: Taiwan overtook India to become the world’s fifth-largest equity market by market capitalisation on Monday, extending a powerful rally driven by enthusiasm around artificial intelligence-related stocks. According to Bloomberg data, Taiwan’s total market value climbed to $4.95 trillion ($4,952.22 billion), edging past India’s market capitalisation of $4.92 trillion ($4,920.51 billion). Only the stock markets of the United States, China, Japan and Hong Kong are currently larger than Taiwan’s in terms of overall market value. Stock market recommendations: AIA Engineering, State Bank of India (SBI), Hindustan Copper are the top stocks to buy on May 27, 2026, according to Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares.

articles.title: ODI tickets worth Rs 52.36 lakh went unsold: Khanna
articles.description: "Tickets worth Rupees 52,36,000 were unsold. Why all the tickets were not sold-out? DDCA started online ticket sale quite late that too without proper advertisement," Khanna claimed in a media release.

Candidates can now download their scorecards from the official KTET website using their registration details. This exam serves as an eligibility test for aspiring teachers in Kerala schools.

articles.title: DRDO CEPTAM 11 Tier 2 exam 2026 to be held on June 15; city intimation slip and admit card soon at drdo.gov.in
articles.description: The Defence Research and Development Organisation has announced the Tier 2 Computer-Based Test for the CEPTAM-11 recruitment drive. This crucial examination for Senior Technical Assistant-B and Technician-A posts is scheduled for June 15, 2026. Candidates shortlisted from Tier 1 will soon receive exam city intimation slips and subsequently their admit cards. Stay updated on drdo.gov.in.

articles.title: Mumbai UG admissions 2026: Professional courses surge as BA, BSc cut-offs drop across colleges
articles.description: Mumbai colleges are witnessing a significant shift in undergraduate admissions. Traditional BA and BSc courses are seeing lower cut-offs, while professional, career-oriented programmes in finance, healthcare, and technology are experiencing a surge in demand and competition. Colleges are adapting by introducing new specialized courses and skill-based training to meet student aspirations and industry needs.
articles.full_text: Mumbai colleges are witnessing a significant shift in undergraduate admissions. Traditional BA and BSc courses are seeing lower cut-offs, while professional, career-oriented programmes in finance, healthcare, and technology are experiencing a surge in demand and competition. Colleges are adapting by introducing new specialized courses and skill-based training to meet student aspirations and industry needs.

articles.title: 'Varun Salman nahi ban sakta': Abhijeet SLAMS 'Chunnari' remix
articles.description: Original 'Chunnari Chunnari' singer Abhijeet Bhattacharya has slammed Varun Dhawan's upcoming film's recreation of the iconic song. Bhattacharya criticized Dhawan for relying on 'second-hand' films and old hits, stating the remake degrades the original's legacy. He revealed makers never contacted him, and he's relieved he wasn't involved. Read on to know more in detail.

articles.title: Gangai Amaran slams rumours about Ilaiyaraaja
articles.description: Veteran musician Gangai Amaran vehemently refuted rumors about his brother, legendary composer Ilaiyaraaja, living alone and unsupported. Speaking at an album launch, Amaran expressed pain over false claims, emphasizing Ilaiyaraaja's peaceful, music-focused life and his sons' connection. He urged respect for the artist's personal space, stating, 'Let him live the way he wants.'

articles.title: THESE Malayalam and Tamil stars faced restrictions and bans
articles.description: Industry bans and restrictions are a recurring issue in Indian cinema, extending beyond Bollywood. Malayalam actors Shane Nigam and Sreenath Bhasi faced recent non-cooperation notices over alleged misconduct and production issues, while Tamil actor Vadivelu endured a prolonged dispute. Legendary actor Thilakan also spoke out against industry power groups, highlighting the persistent challenges faced by artists.
